prompt: Aerial oblique photograph of a dramatic mountain landscape at golden hour. Multiple narrow stream channels are carved into the mountainside, each visibly flowing upward within their own local valley — the water clearly moving toward higher ground within each channel due to the specific contour of each small depression. Yet the overall mountain slope is unambiguously descending from left to right across the full frame — the macro landscape clearly falls away. Both the local upward flow within each channel and the global downward slope of the mountain are simultaneously visible and unambiguous in the same image. The lighting emphasizes the contradictory topography — warm raking sidelight reveals the texture of both the small channels and the large slope. Hyperrealistic landscape photography, large format, extreme clarity, no atmospheric haze obscuring the contradiction. Negative: no text, no labels, no arrows, no diagrams, no people, no annotations, no charts, no mathematical elements
visual_logic: Streams flow visibly upward within their own channels, yet the mountain descends overall. Local and global trends are simultaneously true and contradictory, felt spatially without any literal representation.
